lockingPolicy.lockReason: Bugfix-fastlane MV3 test-harness flake fix; the deterministic badge-matrix e2e proof (20/20 separate cold invocations + full-suite 11/11) drives certification and is locked once green.

tdd.redGreenEvidence: report.md '### Before Fix (RED)' and '### After Fix (GREEN)': BEFORE the triggerDrain() readiness guard, the badge-matrix test failed ~17% of separate cold ./smackerel.sh test e2e-ext invocations (2 of 12, with chrome.alarms undefined at fixtures.ts:192); AFTER, it passes 20/20 separate cold invocations and the full suite passes 11/11. The 'test' is the e2e test; the regression contract is the >=10x cold-green proof of the now-robust helper (delivered 20x). Reverting the in-evaluate chrome.alarms.create readiness poll restores the BEFORE red.

scenarios.title: The badge-matrix sideload smoke is deterministic across cold MV3 service-worker spin-ups (triggerDrain waits for the chrome.alarms binding before firing the drain alarm)

linkedTests.function: badge matrix: an unconfigured install shows SETUP after a drain attempt

scenarios.liveTestExpectation: Run in real headless Chromium (--headless=new) against the REAL built extension, the badge-matrix test calls the now-robust triggerDrain() helper, which (inside the sw.evaluate worker context) waits up to 5s/50ms for `typeof chrome.alarms.create === 'function'` before calling chrome.alarms.create(DRAIN_ALARM). The unconfigured worker's gateConfig() fails and sets the SETUP badge; expect(badge).toBe('SETUP') holds. Proven non-flaky across 20 separate cold ./smackerel.sh test e2e-ext invocations (>=10 required) and a full-suite 11/11 run. NOT request interception; no Playwright retries; assertion unchanged.
